primary_source: Whittaker, 1903. On the partial differential equations of mathematical physics. Math. Annalen 57:333-355. DOI: 10.1007/BF01444290.
title: QA Whittaker Rational Direction S^1 FAIL [266] - HARD-rule violation: d declared independently of b+e
_fixture_purpose: FAIL fixture: declares an explicit override claiming d=10 for seed (b=1, e=2). The HARD rule from CLAUDE.md and memory/feedback_raw_vs_mod_derived.md requires raw d=b+e (no independent assignment, no mod reduction in element computation). For (b=1, e=2), the only legal d is 1+2=3. Declaring d=10 violates the QA construction; validator must produce a WRD_HARD error rejecting the claim.
source_attribution: (Whittaker, 1903) On the partial differential equations of mathematical physics, Math. Annalen 57:333-355, DOI: 10.1007/BF01444290.

witnesses.name: hard_rule_violation
witnesses._note: Validator must reject claimed_d=10 because raw d=b+e=3 for any (b=1, e=2)

fail_ledger.rationale: Element computation must use raw d=b+e per CLAUDE.md HARD rule. Declaring d=10 for (b=1, e=2) where b+e=3 is a Theorem NT-class violation (modifies derived coordinate); validator's claimed_d_overrides check produces WRD_HARD failure.
